content_en: The glorious Quraan says, {Yes, verily in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find tranquility.} (*274) HEALTHY PHYSICAL SPORTS: One of the best alternatives to music and its attendant evils is the engagement in healthy physical sports which brings benefit by developing physical and mental acumen. The Prophet (upon whom be peace and blessings) has encouraged certain sports, among them swimming, horseback riding and the art of marksmanship. (*275) The martial arts is another important field for developing physical and mental prowess. CONCLUSION It is trusted that what has preceded constitutes for the reader a sufficiently explicit analysis and exposition of the Islamic ruling regarding music and song, leaving him/her with a perfectly clear understanding of this serious issue. It is hoped that many of the common misconceptions regarding music and song have been addressed and removed in a lucid, convincing manner, resulting in surety and tranquility for the reader's soul and conscience. I pray that this is {a plain statement to men, a guidance and instruction to those who, fearing Allah, ward off evil.} (*276) THE END Footnotes: (*238)Except for the daff, the simple hand drum, which is allowed for use by women and children on specific occasions mentioned by the authentic sunnah. This has been clarified by a previous section of this work, entitled "Examples of Occasions Specified by the Sunnah." (*239)Such as flutes, horns, trumpets, accordions, saxophones, trombones, etc. (*240)Including guitars, violins, banjos, harps, sitars, lutes, basses, cellos, etc. (*241)Such as gongs, cymbals, bass drums, bongos, congas, tambourines, etc. (*242)For example, the piano, which combines percussion and string instrumentation. (*243)A type of guitar common to Arab lands. (*244)The complete text of these hadeeths has preceded in Arabic along with their translations in the second section of this study under the title, "Critical Analysis of the hadith Literature." (*245)Its size, according to common usage or custom ('urf), varies approximately between twenty and thirty-five centimeters in diameter for the skin. The width of the circular wooden frame upon which it is strung is about a finger's length. Exceeding this stipulated size would result in a louder sound, which in turn exceeds the instrument's basically limited percussion potentiality. Conversely, a much smaller size diminishes its basic sound capability, and thus, the objective of "announcing" the wedding feast through its sound would not be realized. As it is said, "The best of affairs are intermediate" - in the middle ground. (*246)The term daff literally means the (one) side of a thing or its surface. For details, see Lisaanul Arab, vol. 9, pp 104-106. (*247)See Kaffur Ra'aa, pp. 94-95. (*248)These principles were mentioned in the previous section and are also applicable here. (*249)The width of four finger clinched together (which is basically the same) according to various authentic narrations of Iman Al-Bukhaari, Abu Dawood and others. The actual finger's length varies according to each individual, but an average finger length of the middle finger (the longest one), is about 8-9 centimeters. (*250)Threads made from minutely thin, but pure gold wires, woven together to make a garment. (*251)See Al-Bardeesi's Usoolul Fiqh, p. 240 and Abdul-Qaadir Ataa's Haadha Halaal wa Haadha Haraam, p. 211. (*252)Because by its very nature the exception (mustathna) differs from the original ruling to which it is an exception. (*253)In preceding texts of the authenic sunnah. (*254)The kooban is a drum with skin on bothsides. The tabl is similar except it is usually larger in size. And the tablah has skin only at the top, with a long, open, hollow base made from wood, metal or clay. (*255)According to the principle that whatever is coupled with a prohibited thing becomes prohibited. (*256)Such as the 'Eed festival, wedding celebrations, etc. (*257)Such as those songs which describe sex, wine-drinking or any immoral subjects. (*258)This occurs when the singer purposely manipulates his/her voice in a throbbing, titillating manner which arouses sexual passions. (*259)One of the rare geniuses of the Islamic sciences, nicknamed "Sultanul Ulamaa" (the Monarch of Scholars). He definitely deserves this title. He was born in Damascus (577 H./1181 C.E.) and died in Cairo (660 H./1262 C.E.). For details, see Mu'jamul Muallifeen, vol. 5, pp. 249-250. (*260)The immediate refernce inthis passage is to the practice of some Sufis who dance according to a certain method as a so-called "spiritual" exercise. However, what has been said here applies generally to all dancing by males regardless of whether it's done for pleasure or for "worship." (*261)In variuos authentic traditions narrated by Al-Bukhaari, Muslim, et. al. (*262)Quoted from Kaffur Ra'aa, p 73. (*263)Quoted from the tafseer, Roohul Ma'aani, vol. 21, p. 71. (*264)In loose clothing from above the chest to below the knee, at the very minimum. (*265)Such as occurs during belly-dancing or various western dances such as soul, rock and funk. (*266)Just as is the case of wine, whose drinking, production, sale and even carrying is forbidden. (*267)By the proper authorities. See footnote no. 112. (*268)Unless he listens to it with the intent to hear it and seek pleasure in it. (*269)surah Al-Muddaththir, 74:38. (*270)See his foreword to The Glorious Quran, p. 3. (*271)Related by Iman Al-Bukkaari. The Quraan is to be melodiously chanted or sung, but not according to one's personal style dictated by fancy. The Quraan must be recited according to the rules of tajweed, the precise science which details the rules for Quraanic recitation.
content_id: Al-Quran yang mulia berfirman, {Ingatlah, hanya dengan mengingat Allah-lah hati menjadi tenteram.} OLAHRAGA FISIK YANG SEHAT: Salah satu alternatif terbaik bagi musik dan kejahatan-kejahatan yang menyertainya adalah keterlibatan dalam olahraga fisik yang sehat yang memberikan manfaat dengan mengembangkan kecerdasan fisik dan mental. Nabi ﷺ telah mendorong olahraga-olahraga tertentu, di antaranya berenang, berkuda, dan seni memanah. Seni beladiri adalah bidang penting lainnya untuk mengembangkan kekuatan fisik dan mental. KESIMPULAN Dipercaya bahwa apa yang telah dikemukakan merupakan bagi pembaca suatu analisis dan uraian yang cukup jelas tentang hukum Islam mengenai musik dan lagu, meninggalkan beliau dengan pemahaman yang sempurna jelas tentang masalah serius ini. Diharapkan bahwa banyak kesalahpahaman umum mengenai musik dan lagu telah dibahas dan dihilangkan dengan cara yang gamblang dan meyakinkan, menghasilkan kepastian dan ketentraman bagi jiwa dan hati nurani pembaca. Penulis berdoa semoga ini adalah {suatu penjelasan yang terang bagi manusia, dan menjadi petunjuk serta pelajaran bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa.} SELESAI Catatan kaki: (*238) Kecuali daff, rebana sederhana, yang dibolehkan digunakan oleh wanita dan anak-anak pada kesempatan-kesempatan tertentu yang disebutkan oleh sunnah yang sahih. Hal ini telah dijelaskan oleh bagian sebelumnya dari karya ini, berjudul "Contoh-Contoh Kesempatan yang Ditentukan oleh Sunnah." (*239) Seperti seruling, terompet, trompet, akordeon, saksofon, trombon, dan sebagainya. (*240) Termasuk gitar, biola, banjo, harpa, sitar, kecapi, bass, cello, dan sebagainya. (*241) Seperti gong, simbal, gendang bass, bongo, conga, tamborin, dan sebagainya. (*242) Misalnya, piano, yang menggabungkan perkusi dan instrumen senar. (*243) Sejenis gitar yang umum di tanah-tanah Arab. (*244) Teks lengkap dari hadis-hadis ini telah dikemukakan sebelumnya dalam bahasa Arab beserta terjemahannya dalam bagian kedua dari kajian ini di bawah judul "Analisis Kritis terhadap Literatur Hadis." (*245) Ukurannya, menurut penggunaan atau kebiasaan umum ('urf), bervariasi kira-kira antara dua puluh hingga tiga puluh lima sentimeter diameternya untuk kulit. Lebar bingkai kayu melingkar yang ditali adalah sekitar sepanjang satu jari. Melampaui ukuran yang ditetapkan ini akan menghasilkan suara yang lebih keras, yang pada gilirannya melampaui potensi perkusi dasar instrumen yang pada dasarnya terbatas. Sebaliknya, ukuran yang jauh lebih kecil mengurangi kemampuan suara dasar instrumen tersebut, dan dengan demikian, tujuan "mengumumkan" pesta pernikahan melalui suaranya tidak akan terwujud. Sebagaimana dikatakan, "Sebaik-baik perkara adalah yang pertengahan" - berada di tengah-tengah. (*246) Istilah daff secara harfiah berarti (satu) sisi sesuatu atau permukaannya. Untuk perincian, lihat Lisan al-Arab, vol. 9, hlm. 104-106. (*247) Lihat Kaff ar-Ra'a, hlm. 94-95. (*248) Prinsip-prinsip ini disebutkan dalam bagian sebelumnya dan juga berlaku di sini. (*249) Lebar empat jari yang dirapatkan (yang pada dasarnya sama) menurut berbagai riwayat sahih Imam Al-Bukhari, Abu Dawud, dan lainnya. Panjang jari sebenarnya bervariasi menurut masing-masing individu, tetapi rata-rata panjang jari dari jari tengah (yang terpanjang), adalah sekitar 8-9 sentimeter. (*250) Benang-benang yang terbuat dari kawat emas murni yang sangat tipis, ditenun bersama untuk membuat pakaian. (*251) Lihat Usul al-Fiqh karya Al-Bardeesi, hlm. 240, dan Hadha Halal wa Hadha Haram karya Abdul-Qadir 'Ata, hlm. 211. (*252) Karena berdasarkan sifatnya, pengecualian (mustatsna) berbeda dari hukum asli yang menjadi pengecualiannya. (*253) Dalam teks-teks sunnah yang sahih sebelumnya. (*254) Kubah adalah gendang dengan kulit di kedua sisi. Tabl serupa kecuali biasanya lebih besar ukurannya. Dan tablah memiliki kulit hanya di bagian atas, dengan alas yang panjang, berlubang, terbuat dari kayu, logam, atau tanah liat. (*255) Menurut prinsip bahwa apa pun yang digabungkan dengan hal yang terlarang menjadi terlarang. (*256) Seperti festival Ied, perayaan pernikahan, dan sebagainya. (*257) Seperti lagu-lagu yang menggambarkan seks, minum-minuman keras, atau subjek-subjek yang tidak bermoral. (*258) Ini terjadi ketika penyanyi dengan sengaja memanipulasi suaranya dengan cara yang berdenyut dan menggairahkan yang membangkitkan nafsu seksual. (*259) Salah satu dari langka para jenius ilmu-ilmu Islam, dijuluki "Sultanul Ulama" (Penguasa Para Ulama). Beliau benar-benar layak mendapatkan gelar ini. Beliau lahir di Damaskus (577 H./1181 M.) dan wafat di Kairo (660 H./1262 M.). Untuk perincian, lihat Mu'jam al-Mu'allifin, vol. 5, hlm. 249-250. (*260) Referensi langsung dalam bagian ini adalah pada praktik beberapa kaum Sufi yang menari menurut metode tertentu sebagai latihan "spiritual" yang dianggap. Namun, apa yang telah dikatakan di sini berlaku secara umum untuk semua menari oleh laki-laki tanpa memandang apakah itu dilakukan untuk kesenangan atau untuk "ibadah." (*261) Dalam berbagai riwayat sahih yang diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari, Muslim, dan lainnya. (*262) Dikutip dari Kaff ar-Ra'a, hlm. 73. (*263) Dikutip dari tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani, vol. 21, hlm. 71. (*264) Dalam pakaian yang longgar dari atas dada hingga di bawah lutut, setidaknya. (*265) Seperti yang terjadi selama tari perut atau berbagai tarian Barat seperti soul, rock, dan funk. (*266) Sama seperti halnya dengan khamr, yang meminumnya, memproduksinya, menjualnya, bahkan membawanya adalah terlarang. (*267) Oleh pihak berwenang yang tepat. Lihat catatan kaki no. 112. (*268) Kecuali beliau mendengarkannya dengan niat untuk mendengar dan mencari kesenangan darinya. (*269) Surah Al-Muddatstsir, 74:38. (*270) Lihat kata pengantarnya untuk The Glorious Quran, hlm. 3. (*271) Diriwayatkan oleh Imam Al-Bukhari. Al-Quran harus dilantunkan atau dinyanyikan secara merdu, tetapi tidak sesuai dengan gaya pribadi yang didikte oleh keinginan. Al-Quran harus dibaca sesuai dengan aturan-aturan tajwid, ilmu yang tepat yang merinci aturan-aturan untuk bacaan Al-Quran.
content_ar: The glorious Quraan says, {Yes, verily in the remembrance of الله do hearts find tranquility.} HEALTHY PHYSICAL SPORTS: One of the best alternatives to الموسيقى and its attendant evils is the engagement in healthy physical sports which brings benefit by developing physical and mental acumen. النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (upon whom be peace and blessings) has encouraged certain sports, among them swimming, horseback riding and the art of marksmanship. The martial arts is another important field for developing physical and mental prowess. CONCLUSION It is trusted that what has preceded constitutes for the reader a sufficiently explicit analysis and exposition of the الإسلامي ruling regarding الموسيقى and الغناء, leaving him/her with a perfectly clear understanding of this serious issue. It is hoped that many of the common misconceptions regarding الموسيقى and الغناء have been addressed and removed in a lucid, convincing manner, resulting in surety and tranquility for the reader's soul and conscience. I pray that this is {a plain statement to men, a guidance and instruction to those who, fearing الله, ward off evil.} THE END Footnotes: Except for the daff, the simple hand drum, which is allowed for use by women and children on specific occasions mentioned by the صحيح السنة. This has been clarified by a previous section of this work, entitled "Examples of Occasions Specified by السنة النبوية." Such as flutes, horns, trumpets, accordions, saxophones, trombones, etc. Including guitars, violins, banjos, harps, sitars, lutes, basses, cellos, etc. Such as gongs, cymbals, bass drums, bongos, congas, tambourines, etc. For example, the piano, which combines percussion and string instrumentation. A type of guitar common to Arab lands. The complete text of these hadeeths has preceded in Arabic along with their translations in the second section of this study under the title, "Critical Analysis of الحديث النبوي الشريف Literature." Its size, according to common usage or custom ('urf), varies approximately between twenty and thirty-five centimeters in diameter for the skin. The width of the circular wooden frame upon which it is strung is about a finger's length. Exceeding this stipulated size would result in a louder sound, which in turn exceeds the instrument's basically limited percussion potentiality. Conversely, a much smaller size diminishes its basic sound capability, and thus, the objective of "announcing" the wedding feast through its sound would not be realized. As it is said, "The best of affairs are intermediate" - in the middle ground. The term daff literally means the (one) side of a thing or its surface. For details, see Lisaanul Arab, vol. 9, pp 104-106. See Kaffur Ra'aa, pp. 94-95. These principles were mentioned in the previous section and are also applicable here. The width of four finger clinched together (which is basically the same) according to various صحيح narrations of Iman Al-Bukhaari, Abu Dawood and others. The actual finger's length varies according to each individual, but an average finger length of the middle finger (the longest one), is about 8-9 centimeters. Threads made from minutely thin, but pure gold wires, woven together to make a garment. See Al-Bardeesi's Usoolul الفقه, p. 240 and Abdul-Qaadir Ataa's Haadha Halaal wa Haadha Haraam, p. 211. Because by its very nature the exception (mustathna) differs from the original ruling to which it is an exception. In preceding texts of the authenic السنة. The kooban is a drum with skin on bothsides. The tabl is similar except it is usually larger in size. And the tablah has skin only at the top, with a long, open, hollow base made from wood, metal or clay. According to the principle that whatever is coupled with a prohibited thing becomes prohibited. Such as the 'Eed festival, wedding celebrations, etc. Such as those الغناء which describe sex, wine-drinking or any الفحش والفساد الأخلاقي subjects. This occurs when the singer purposely manipulates his/her voice in a throbbing, titillating manner which arouses sexual passions. One of the rare geniuses of the الإسلامي sciences, nicknamed "Sultanul Ulamaa" (the Monarch of العلماء). He definitely deserves this title. He was born in Damascus (577 H./1181 C.E.) and died in Cairo (660 H./1262 C.E.). For details, see Mu'jamul Muallifeen, vol. 5, pp. 249-250. The immediate refernce inthis passage is to the practice of some Sufis who dance according to a certain method as a so-called "spiritual" exercise. However, what has been said here applies generally to all dancing by males regardless of whether it's done for pleasure or for "worship." In variuos صحيح traditions روى by Al-Bukhaari, المسلم, et. al. Quoted from Kaffur Ra'aa, p 73. Quoted from the tafseer, Roohul Ma'aani, vol. 21, p. 71. In loose clothing from above the chest to below the knee, at the very minimum. Such as occurs during belly-dancing or various western dances such as soul, rock and funk. Just as is the case of wine, whose drinking, production, sale and even carrying is محرم. By the proper authorities. See footnote no. 112. Unless he listens to it with the intent to hear it and seek pleasure in it. surah Al-Muddaththir, 74:38. See his foreword to The Glorious القرآن الكريم, p. 3. Related by Iman Al-Bukkaari. The Quraan is to be melodiously chanted or sung, but not according to one's personal style dictated by fancy. The Quraan must be recited according to the rules of tajweed, the precise science which details the rules for Quraanic recitation.
